skill.name: firebase-security-rules-auditor
skill.description: A skill to evaluate how secure Firestore security rules are. Use this when Firestore security rules are updated to ensure that the generated rules are extremely secure and robust.
skill.summary: Audits Firestore security rules against a penetration testing checklist to detect privilege escalation, data leaks, and misconfigurations.
skill.author: firebase

security_audit.summary: All 8 static analysis findings are false positives. Backticks on line 19 are markdown inline code formatting for hasOnly() and diff(), not shell execution. Lines 3 and 41 contain metadata text and JSON example output with no cryptographic code. Lines 14 and 19 describe legitimate security audit methodology for a security auditing skill, not reconnaissance activity. The skill is a purely instructional prompt template with no executable code, network access, or file system operations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown backticks misidentified as shell execution
dangerous_patterns.description: Markdown inline code formatting on line 19 (hasOnly() and diff()) triggered external_commands detection. These are documentation formatting, not shell backtick execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: Security methodology misidentified as reconnaissance
dangerous_patterns.description: Audit checklist items describing how to test for update bypass and privilege escalation were flagged as system reconnaissance. These are legitimate security auditing instructions for the skill's intended purpose.

dangerous_patterns.title: Metadata text misidentified as weak cryptography
dangerous_patterns.description: Frontmatter description field and JSON example output triggered cryptographic algorithm detection. No cryptographic code exists in the file.

content.user_title: Audit Firebase Security Rules for Vulnerabilities
content.value_statement: Firestore security rules are difficult to configure correctly and often contain vulnerabilities. This skill acts as a security auditor to systematically evaluate your rules against penetration testing criteria and identify critical weaknesses before deployment.
actual_capabilities: Evaluates Firestore security rules against a structured 6-point audit checklist covering update bypass, authority source, business logic, storage abuse, type safety, and field-level security
Detects privilege escalation paths including users creating valid documents then updating them into malicious states
Identifies missing type safety checks, absent size limits, and insufficient ownership verification
Assesses admin bootstrapping security including email verification requirements and escalation risk
Returns scored assessments from 1 (critical) to 5 (secure) with detailed findings and actionable recommendations
Examines business logic consistency between create and update rules to prevent authorization gaps
limitations: Only evaluates Firestore security rules and does not cover Firebase Realtime Database or Cloud Storage rules
Cannot test rules against a live Firebase environment or emulator
Relies on the accuracy and completeness of the rules provided by the user
Does not assess compliance with specific regulatory frameworks such as GDPR or SOC 2

use_cases.title: Pre-Deployment Security Check
use_cases.description: A Firebase developer wants to verify their Firestore security rules for vulnerabilities before deploying to a production environment.

use_cases.title: Compliance Audit Support
use_cases.description: A security engineer reviews Firestore rules as part of a security audit and needs systematic vulnerability detection across all rule paths.

use_cases.title: Startup Launch Readiness
use_cases.description: A startup CTO wants to confirm their Firebase backend does not expose user data before a public product launch.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Security Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit my Firestore security rules for basic vulnerabilities. Here are my rules: [paste your Firestore security rules]
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user performing an initial security check

prompt_templates.title: Update Bypass Detection
prompt_templates.prompt: I am concerned about update bypass vulnerabilities. Review my create and update rules for privilege escalation paths where a user could create a valid document then update it to gain unauthorized access: [paste your rules]
prompt_templates.scenario: Developer focused on authorization gap analysis

prompt_templates.title: Role-Based Access Control Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit my security rules for role-based access control issues. Check for self-assigned roles, missing ownership checks, and admin bootstrapping security. Here are my rules: [paste your rules]
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user with complex role hierarchy and admin privileges

prompt_templates.title: Full Penetration Test
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a complete security penetration test on my Firestore rules. Run all 6 audit checklist items including update bypass, authority source validation, business logic review, storage abuse, type safety, and field-level security: [paste your rules]

output_examples.input: Audit these rules: allow read, write: if request.auth != null; for the /users/{userId} path
output: Finding: Missing ownership check - any authenticated user can read or write any user document
Recommendation: Add ownership check using request.auth.uid == userId to restrict access to document owners

output_examples.input: Check these rules for update bypass: allow create: if request.resource.data.role is string; allow update: if true;
output: Finding: Update bypass detected - users can create a document with a valid role then update it to change their role to admin
Recommendation: Add matching role validation to the update rule to prevent privilege escalation

best_practices: Always provide complete security rules rather than partial snippets for thorough analysis
Run audits after every rules change as part of your deployment pipeline
Review each finding's recommendation and test fixes in the Firebase emulator before deploying
anti_patterns: Do not trust the audit blindly without testing rules in the Firebase emulator against actual queries
Do not ignore findings rated as minor since they can combine into critical vulnerabilities
Do not audit rules without first understanding your application's business logic and data model

faq.question: What Firebase services does this skill audit?
faq.answer: This skill audits Firestore security rules. It does not cover Firebase Realtime Database, Cloud Storage, or other Firebase services.

faq.question: How is the security score calculated?
faq.answer: The score ranges from 1 to 5. Score 1 indicates critical vulnerabilities like data leaks or privilege escalation. Score 5 indicates comprehensive security with strict validation, ownership checks, and proper type safety.

faq.question: Can this skill test rules against a live database?
faq.answer: No. This skill performs static analysis of your rule definitions. Use the Firebase emulator or an automated test suite to validate rules against live data and queries.

faq.question: Does this skill check for regulatory compliance?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on technical security vulnerabilities. It does not check for compliance with specific frameworks such as SOC 2, GDPR, or HIPAA.

faq.question: What is an update bypass vulnerability?
faq.answer: An update bypass occurs when create rules are strict but update rules are permissive. A user can create a valid document that passes create checks, then update it into a malicious or unauthorized state.

faq.question: Can I audit rules for multiple Firestore databases?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide rules for any Firestore database and the audit will evaluate all match blocks and document paths within your rules.
